prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arr, me hearties! Avast ye and lend an ear, for I'll spin ye a tale of the vast blue yonder, as true as the gold in me chest. Why be the sky as blue as the Caribbean waters on a fair day? 'Tis on account of the sun's mighty rays, them what get all tangled and scattered by the wee particles in the air, like grapeshot from a cannon!

Ye see, the sun's light be a rainbow of colors, but when it strikes the atmosphere's misty veil, the blue light—bein' the shortest and feistiest of the lot—gets flung about every which way, whilst the red and yellow scurvy dogs sail straight on. So, up we gaze and behold that brilliant blue, as if Poseidon's own trident painted the heavens!

Shiver me timbers, 'tis a marvel fit for a captain's log, and no mere fancy. Fair winds to ye, and may yer horizons ever be as vast and blue! Arr!
